description_long: This is a large, single-story log cabin constructed primarily from interlocking wooden logs, featuring a broad, multi-faceted hip roof with deep overhangs and several skylights or vents. The exterior walls are composed of rounded logs, giving the structure a rustic and robust appearance, while the foundation is finished with a stone or concrete base. Multiple rectangular windows are symmetrically arranged along the visible facades, providing ample natural light to the interior. The cabin includes a covered porch area supported by log columns, extending from the main structure and offering sheltered outdoor space. The roof is clad in dark brown shingles, and the eaves project significantly, enhancing weather protection and architectural character. The overall design is reminiscent of traditional log cabins, suited for both rural and upscale suburban environments.
description: A large, rustic log cabin with a broad hip roof, deep overhangs, and multiple windows, featuring a covered porch and stone foundation.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main facade with three large windows, log construction, and the prominent overhanging roof.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the extended roof, two skylights, and a side deck area with fewer visible windows.
description_view_2: Side view displaying the covered porch, a large window, and the log wall details beneath the expansive roof.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with a centered porch, railing, and symmetrical window arrangement under the wide roof.
attribute_roof_type: hip roof with multiple facets, deep overhangs, and several skylights or vents; covered in dark shingles
attribute_window_arrangement: Multiple rectangular windows symmetrically arranged in groups of three on the main facades, with additional single or paired windows on the sides; all windows are medium to large in size and horizontally aligned
attribute_door_placement: One main exterior door located under the covered porch on the front facade, aligned with the porch structure; no other doors visible
